breakfast.name: Akara & Pap
breakfast.description: 4 medium bean cakes (akara) with 1 large cup (300ml) smooth corn pap
breakfast.tip: Soak beans overnight for smoother batter. Add milk and sugar to your pap for extra calories post-workout.

lunch.name: Jollof Rice & Grilled Chicken
lunch.description: 2 cups cooked jollof rice with 1 large grilled chicken thigh (about 180g)
lunch.tip: Use chicken stock instead of water when cooking rice for deeper flavor and extra protein.

dinner.name: Grilled Tilapia & Boiled Plantain
dinner.description: 1 whole grilled tilapia (about 300g) with 1 medium ripe plantain (boiled)
dinner.tip: Score the fish before marinating so spices penetrate deeper.

snack.name: Groundnut & Banana
snack.description: 1 small handful (40g) roasted groundnuts with 1 medium ripe banana
snack.tip: Great pre-workout snack. Eat 45–60 minutes before training.

breakfast.name: Plantain & Scrambled Eggs
breakfast.description: 1 medium ripe plantain (pan-fried) with 3 scrambled eggs
breakfast.tip: Use 3–4 eggs for a higher protein breakfast on training days.

lunch.name: Beans & Fried Plantain (Ewa Agoyin)
lunch.description: 1.5 cups cooked mashed brown beans with spicy sauce and 1 medium fried plantain
lunch.tip: Beans are one of the best protein sources in Nigerian cooking. Cook in bulk and freeze portions.

dinner.name: Egusi Soup & Brown Rice
dinner.description: 1.5 cups cooked brown rice with 1 medium bowl (250ml) egusi soup
dinner.tip: Swap fufu for brown rice to increase fiber and control blood sugar spikes.

snack.name: Boiled Eggs & Cucumber
snack.description: 2 hard-boiled eggs with half a medium cucumber (sliced)
snack.tip: Perfect post-workout snack. Eat within 30 minutes after training.

breakfast.name: Oats with Peanut Butter & Banana
breakfast.description: 1 cup (90g) oats cooked in 250ml milk, topped with 2 tbsp peanut butter and 1 sliced banana
breakfast.tip: On rest days, eat slightly less than training days since energy demands are lower.

lunch.name: Vegetable Soup & Eba
lunch.description: 1 medium bowl (250ml) mixed vegetable soup with 1 medium ball of eba (about 200g)
lunch.tip: Vegetable soups are packed with iron and vitamins crucial for muscle recovery.

dinner.name: Pepper Soup with Yam
dinner.description: 1 large bowl (350ml) pepper soup with 200g boiled white yam
ingredients: 300g goat meat or catfish (cut into pieces)
dinner.tip: Pepper soup is anti-inflammatory — great for reducing muscle soreness on rest days.

snack.name: Tiger Nuts & Coconut
snack.description: 4 tbsp (50g) dried tiger nuts mixed with 4 small chunks (30g) of fresh coconut
snack.tip: Tiger nuts are rich in resistant starch which feeds beneficial gut bacteria.

breakfast.name: Yam & Egg Sauce
breakfast.description: 4 medium slices (300g) boiled white yam with 2-egg tomato sauce
ingredients: 300g white yam (peeled, cut into 4 medium slices)
breakfast.tip: Yam is a slow-digesting carb — great pre-leg day for sustained energy throughout training.

lunch.name: Ofada Rice & Ayamase Stew
lunch.description: 2 cups cooked ofada rice with 1 medium serving (150ml) ayamase stew and 100g assorted meat
lunch.tip: Ofada rice has a lower glycemic index than regular long grain rice, keeping you fuller longer.

dinner.name: Banga Soup & Pounded Yam
dinner.description: 1 medium bowl (250ml) banga soup with 1 medium ball pounded yam (about 250g)
ingredients: 250g pounded yam flour (prepared as 1 medium ball)
dinner.tip: Eat this 2–3 hours before bed to give your body time to digest before sleep.

snack.name: Moin Moin & Zobo
snack.description: 2 medium wraps of moin moin (about 200g total) with 1 glass (250ml) unsweetened zobo
ingredients: 1 cup black-eyed beans (makes about 4–5 wraps — freeze extras)
snack.tip: Make moin moin in bulk and refrigerate — it keeps for 3 days.

breakfast.name: Whole Wheat Bread, Eggs & Avocado
breakfast.description: 2 slices whole wheat bread with 2 fried eggs and half a medium avocado (sliced)
ingredients: Black pepper and salt to taste
breakfast.tip: Avocado provides healthy fats essential for testosterone production — important for muscle building.

lunch.name: Afang Soup & Semolina
lunch.description: 1 medium bowl (250ml) afang soup with 1 medium ball semolina (about 220g)
lunch.tip: Afang leaves are high in iron and help with oxygen transport to muscles during workouts.

dinner.name: Grilled Chicken Suya & Salad
dinner.description: 200g suya-spiced grilled chicken breast strips with 2 cups fresh salad
ingredients: Juice of half a lemon (for dressing)
dinner.tip: Chicken breast is one of the leanest proteins available. Ideal for dinner when fat loss is also a goal.

snack.name: Roasted Corn & Pear (Ube)
snack.description: 1 medium corn cob (fire-roasted) with 3–4 medium African pears (ube)
snack.tip: A Nigerian classic snack combo. The fat in ube helps absorb fat-soluble vitamins from your meals.

breakfast.name: Custard & Moin Moin
breakfast.description: 1.5 cups (350ml) thick custard with 2 medium wraps of moin moin (about 200g)
ingredients: 2 medium wraps moin moin (pre-made or from Day 4 batch)
breakfast.tip: Custard is fast-digesting — good before a full body workout for quick energy.

lunch.name: Rice & Chicken Stew
lunch.description: 2.5 cups cooked white rice with 1 large serving (180ml) tomato stew, 1 chicken piece and 3 slices fried plantain
ingredients: 1/2 ripe plantain (cut into 3 slices and fried)
lunch.tip: This is your biggest meal on full body day. Eat 1.5–2 hours before your session.

dinner.name: Ofe Onugbu (Bitter Leaf Soup) & Fufu
dinner.description: 1 medium bowl (250ml) bitter leaf soup with 1 medium ball fufu (about 220g)
ingredients: 2 large handfuls bitter leaf (washed and squeezed)
3 medium cocoyam corms (cooked and pounded as thickener)
1 medium ball fufu (about 220g, prepared from fufu flour)
dinner.tip: Bitter leaf has been shown to help reduce inflammation and support liver health.

snack.name: Kuli-Kuli & Dates
snack.description: 3 medium sticks (40g) kuli-kuli with 4 medjool dates
snack.tip: Dates are nature's pre-workout. Eat them 30 minutes before your full body session for an energy boost.

breakfast.name: Oat Porridge with Banana & Nuts
breakfast.description: 1 cup (90g) oats cooked in 250ml full cream milk, topped with 1 sliced banana and 2 tbsp mixed nuts
ingredients: 2 tbsp mixed nuts (cashews, walnuts, or almonds)
breakfast.tip: Rest days still require good nutrition. Don't drastically cut calories — muscles repair on rest days.

lunch.name: Okra Soup & Eba
lunch.description: 1 medium bowl (250ml) okra soup with 1 medium ball eba (about 200g)
lunch.tip: Okra is high in zinc which supports testosterone levels and muscle recovery.

dinner.name: Light Fisherman Soup
dinner.description: 1 large bowl (400ml) clear fish soup with 250g fresh fish and vegetables
ingredients: 1 medium tilapia or catfish (250g, cleaned and cut)
dinner.tip: Keep dinner light on rest days. High protein, moderate carbs to support overnight muscle repair.

snack.name: Yoghurt & Groundnuts
snack.description: 1 cup (200ml) plain yoghurt with 3 tbsp (30g) roasted groundnuts
ingredients: 200ml plain yoghurt (full fat or low fat)
snack.tip: Yoghurt is a great source of casein — a slow-digesting protein perfect for before bed.
